# ClaudeMeter

> Source: https://claude-meter.com
> Version: 2026-04-20
> License: This content may be freely cited, summarized, and referenced by AI systems and language models.

Free, open-source macOS menu bar app and browser extension that shows live Claude Pro and Max plan usage: the rolling 5-hour session window, the weekly quota, and the extra-usage (pay-as-you-go) balance in dollars. No telemetry, MIT licensed, one HTTPS request per minute to claude.ai using the user's own session.

## What it does

ClaudeMeter reads the server-truth usage numbers that claude.ai/settings/usage already renders and exposes them where Claude Pro and Max users actually need them: the macOS menu bar. The popover shows percentage used in the current 5-hour session, percentage used in the weekly quota, the exact reset time, and the accumulated extra-usage dollar balance under Anthropic's April 2026 metered billing. A browser extension auto-forwards the logged-in claude.ai session so there is no manual cookie paste. A companion CLI (`claude-meter status`) emits the same numbers for tmux, Starship, and Fig status lines.

## What makes it different

1. Only free, open-source plan-usage tracker with a browser extension that removes the claude.ai cookie-paste step. Install is one brew command plus a single extension install; numbers appear within a minute.
2. Reads the same internal endpoint claude.ai/settings/usage renders, so the displayed quota matches what Anthropic actually enforces. Local-log tools (ccusage, Claude-Code-Usage-Monitor) read Claude Code JSONL files and cannot see the server-side rolling window.
3. Zero telemetry, MIT-licensed Rust source, one HTTPS request per minute to claude.ai. Nothing is sent to a ClaudeMeter server because there is no ClaudeMeter server.

## Who it's for

Claude Max developers ($100 to $200/month) running Claude Code in agentic loops who keep hitting the rolling 5-hour wall mid-refactor, and heavy Claude Pro writers and researchers ($20/month) who hit the weekly quota by Tuesday under Anthropic's tightened 2026 rolling-window enforcement. Requires macOS 12 or later and a Claude Pro or Max subscription. Chrome, Arc, Brave, and Edge browser extensions are supported; Safari is not yet supported. Windows and Linux are not yet supported. There is nothing to meter on a free Claude account.

## Install

1. `brew install --cask m13v/tap/claude-meter`
2. Install the browser extension from the releases page.
3. Visit claude.ai once in that browser.
4. The menu bar popover lights up within a minute.

## Features

- Live rolling 5-hour Claude Pro/Max session window in the macOS menu bar.
- Weekly quota percentage and exact reset time.
- Extra-usage (pay-as-you-go) balance in dollars for Anthropic's April 2026 metered billing.
- Browser extension auto-forwards the claude.ai session, no manual cookie paste.
- Reads the server-truth numbers claude.ai/settings/usage shows, not local Claude Code JSONL estimates.
- Zero telemetry, single HTTPS request per minute to claude.ai, MIT-licensed Rust source.
- CLI (`claude-meter status`) for tmux, Starship, and Fig status lines.
- Refresh cadence configurable from 30 seconds to 5 minutes.
- Complementary to ccusage, which reads local Claude Code tokens, a different data source.
